disagreements.narrative_excerpt: Examined SIEM Weekly Correlation Report dated 2026-05-22 (report period 2026-05-15 to 2026-05-21) from Splunk Enterprise Security; five log sources reported healthy heartbeats and correlated events recorded dispositions establishing event outcomes (e.g., CORR-2026-05-16-0042 BENIGN, CORR-2026-05-18-0117 APPROVED, CORR-2026-05-20-0091 BLOCKED).

On-prem: verified via USD20240212 (Identification and Authentication Procedures, v1.4) that organizational users are uniquely identified and authenticated — PIV smart card + PIN for privileged users, username + password + TOTP for standard users, and gMSA for service accounts. Cloud: customer inherits the AWS GovCloud (US) underlying infrastructure layer per the AWS FedRAMP High CRM, with the AWS FedRAMP High SSP referenced as the authoritative source.

Examined Identification and Authentication Procedures (USD20240212) v1.4 §2 and §4.1, which document management of system identifiers by user class (privileged, standard, service account, local console) and PIV card issuance per individual under FIPS 201-3.

Examined USD20240212 Identification and Authentication Procedures §4.1; PIV card termination follows immediately on departure with issuance and termination events recorded in the PIV management system, and §4 documents help-desk reporting and re-verification per USD20240315 before authenticator re-issuance.
